---
title: "timemetric paper code"
output: html_document
date: "2025-10-12"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
rm(list = ls())
library(survival)
library(TimeMetric)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(stringr)
library(pec)
library(randomForestSRC)
```


# Simulation

## population sim

```{r}
### train data

sim_model <- expand.grid(v = 1/c(0.5, 1, 1.5, 2),
                         beta = c(0.5, 0.7, 1, 1.5))
sim_model$model <- 1:dim(sim_model)[1]
apply(sim_model, 1, function(x){
  sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 10000, pi_c = 0.00,
                           v = x[1], beta = x[2]*c(1, -1), 
                           seed = x[3]) -> df
  m.cox <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~., data = df, x = T, y = T)
  return(list(m.cox = m.cox, model = x[3]))
}) -> model_list

### test data
sim_table <- expand.grid(v = 1/c(0.5, 1, 1.5, 2),
                         beta = c(0.5, 0.7, 1, 1.5),
                         index = c(1:100))
sim_table$index <- 1:dim(sim_table)[1]
sim_table <- left_join(sim_table, sim_model, by = c("v", "beta"))
apply(sim_table, 1, function(x){
  sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 3000, pi_c = 0.00,
                           v = x[1], beta = x[2]*c(1, -1), 
                           seed = x[3]) -> df
  return(list(df=df, index = x[3], model = x[4]))
}) -> data_list
```



```{r, message=FALSE}
output_file <- "paper.sim1_table.csv"

lapply(data_list, function(x){
  m.cox.index <- x[["model"]]
  m.cox <- model_list[[m.cox.index]][["m.cox"]]
  covs <- names(m.cox$coefficients)
  pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox, covs = covs, 
                                new_data = x[["df"]], tau = 10e10)
  pam.summary(list(value=pred1), tau = 10e10) -> table_out
  table_out$index <- x[["index"]]
  # append to CSV
  if (!file.exists(output_file)) {
    # if file doesn't exist → write header
    write.csv(table_out, file = output_file, row.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    # if file exists → append without header
    write.table(
      table_out,
      file = output_file,
      sep = ",",
      row.names = FALSE,
      col.names = FALSE,
      append = TRUE
    )
  }
  #print(x[["index"]])
  return(table_out)
}) -> output_list
```


```{r}
# === Define metrics to include and their pretty labels ===
metrics_to_plot <- c("Pseudo_R_square", "Brier Score", 
                     "R_E", "Harrell’s C", "Time Dependent Auc") #"R_sh", 
metric_labels <- list(
  "Pseudo R²",
  "Brier score",
  #expression(R[sh]),
  expression(R[E]),
  "C-index",
  "AUC"
)

# === Load data ===
combine_output <- read_csv("paper.sim1_table.csv") %>%
  left_join(sim_table, by = "index")

# === Compute mean value per setting ===
combine_mean <- combine_output %>%
  filter(Metric %in% metrics_to_plot) %>%
  group_by(Metric, v, beta) %>%
  summarise(value = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  na.omit()

# === Y-axis limits per metric ===
y_limits <- combine_mean %>%
  group_by(Metric) %>%
  summarise(
    ymin = min(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax = max(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Metric = as.character(Metric))

# === Build plots ===
plots_v <- list()
plots_beta <- list()

for (i in seq_along(metrics_to_plot)) {
  m <- metrics_to_plot[i]
  label_m <- metric_labels[[i]]

  lim_row <- y_limits[y_limits$Metric == as.character(m), ]
  ylims   <- c(lim_row$ymin, lim_row$ymax)

  dat_m <- combine_mean %>% filter(Metric == m)

  # --- Row 1: change v (beta = 1) ---
  # --- Row 1: change v (beta = 1) -> BAR PLOT ---
  p1 <- dat_m %>%
    filter(beta == 1) %>%
    mutate(inv_v = round(1 / v, 2),
           x_fac = factor(inv_v)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = x_fac, y = value)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = "1/v", y = label_m) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = ylims) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.17))) +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 9)
    )

  # --- Row 2: change beta (v = 1) ---
  # --- Row 2: change beta (v = 1) -> BAR PLOT ---
  p2 <- dat_m %>%
    filter(v == 1) %>%
    mutate(x_fac = factor(beta, levels = sort(unique(beta)))) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = x_fac, y = value)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(value, 2)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    theme_bw() +
    labs(x = expression(beta), y = label_m) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = ylims) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.1, 0.17))) +
    # optional: show only specific beta ticks if desired
    scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE) +
    theme(
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 9),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 9)
    )

  plots_v[[m]] <- p1
  plots_beta[[m]] <- p2
}

# === Combine rows ===
top_row    <- wrap_plots(plotlist = plots_v, nrow = 2)
bottom_row <- wrap_plots(plotlist = plots_beta, nrow = 2)

# === Row headers ===
row_header <- function(expr_label, hjust) {
  ggplot() +
    theme_void() +
    annotate(
      "text",
      x = 0, y = 1,
      label = expr_label,
      parse = TRUE,
      hjust = hjust, vjust = 1,
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 4.2
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), clip = "off") +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(t = 2, r = 0, b = 4, l = 20) # <-- added left padding
    )
}
hdr_top <- row_header("bold('(b) Vary the variance of event times through ') * 1/v", hjust = 0.203)
hdr_bot <- row_header("bold('(a) Vary the effect size through ') * beta", hjust = 0.3)

row_block_top <- hdr_top / top_row + plot_layout(heights = c(0.08, 1))
row_block_bot <- hdr_bot / bottom_row + plot_layout(heights = c(0.08, 1))

final_plot <- patchwork::wrap_plots(
  plotlist = list(row_block_bot, row_block_top),
  ncol = 1
)

final_plot + plot_annotation(
  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
)
# Save figure
ggsave("paper.fig4.pdf", final_plot, width = 7, height = 7, dpi = 300)
```


## population sim 2 

### simple cox model

```{r, message=FALSE}
sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 10000, pi_c = 0.00,
                         v = 1, beta = 1*c(1, -1), seed = 1234) -> df
m.cox1 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~., data = df, x = T, y = T)
m.cox2 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ x2, data = df, x = T, y = T)
m.ln1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ .,
                  data = df, dist="lognormal", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
m.ln2 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ .,  scale = 2,
                  data = df, dist="lognormal", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
cov1 <- names(m.cox1$coefficients)
cov2 <- names(m.cox2$coefficients)

output_file <- "paper.sim2_table.csv"
t_star <- median(df$time)
lapply(1:100, function(x){
  sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 3000, pi_c = 0.00,
                         v = 1, beta = 1*c(1, -1), seed = x) -> df_test
  pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = cov1,
                                new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  pred2 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox2, covs = cov2,
                                new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = cov1,
                                  new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  pred4 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln2, covs = cov1,
                                  new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  mods <- list(Cox=pred1, Cox.reduce=pred2,
               Ln=pred3, Ln.fix=pred4)
  pam.summary(mods, tau = 10e10, t_star = t_star) -> table_out
  table_out$index <- x
  # append to CSV
  if (!file.exists(output_file)) {
    # if file doesn't exist → write header
    write.csv(table_out, file = output_file, row.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    # if file exists → append without header
    write.table(
      table_out,
      file = output_file,
      sep = ",",
      row.names = FALSE,
      col.names = FALSE,
      append = TRUE
    )
  }
})

```

### complex cox model


```{r, message=FALSE}
sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 10000, pi_c = 0.00,
                         v = 3, beta = c(1,-1,-0.5,0.5), 
                         seed = 1234, nonlinear = TRUE) -> df
m.cox1 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = df, x = T, y = T)
m.cox2 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ x2, data = df, x = T, y = T)
m.ln1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ .,
                  data = df, dist="lognormal", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
m.ln2 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ .,  scale = 5,
                  data = df, dist="lognormal", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
train_rf <- rfsrc(Surv(time, status) ~ ., df) 
cov1 <- names(m.cox1$coefficients)
cov2 <- names(m.cox2$coefficients)

metrics <- c(
    "Pseudo_R_square", "R_square", "L_square",
    "Pseudo_R2_point", "R2_point", "L2_point",
    "Harrell’s C", "Uno’s C", "R_E",
    "Brier Score", "Time Dependent Auc"
  )

output_file <- "paper.sim2_extra_table.csv"
t_star <- median(df$time)
lapply(1:100, function(x){
  sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 3000, pi_c = 0.00,
                         v = 3, beta = c(1,-1,-0.5,0.5), 
                         seed = x, nonlinear = TRUE) -> df_test
  pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = cov1,
                                new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  pred2 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox2, covs = cov2,
                                new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = cov1,
                                  new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  pred4 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln2, covs = cov1,
                                  new_data = df_test, tau = 10e10)
  
  grid <- sort(df_test$time) 
  S_pred <- predictSurvProb(train_rf, newdata = df_test, times = grid)
  pred5 <- list(
    model = NULL, 
    times = df_test$time, 
    surv_prob = S_pred, 
    pred = NULL, 
    status = df_test$status, 
    new_data = NULL, 
    covs = NULL
  )

  mods <- list(Cox = pred1, Cox.reduce = pred2,
               Ln = pred3, Ln.fix = pred4, RF = pred5)
  pam.summary(mods, tau = 10e10, t_star = t_star, metrics = metrics) -> table_out
  table_out$index <- x
  # append to CSV
  if (!file.exists(output_file)) {
    # if file doesn't exist → write header
    write.csv(table_out, file = output_file, row.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    # if file exists → append without header
    write.table(
      table_out,
      file = output_file,
      sep = ",",
      row.names = FALSE,
      col.names = FALSE,
      append = TRUE
    )
  }
})
```

### plot

```{r}
combine_output <- read_csv("paper.sim2_table.csv")

# originals to filter/loop with
metric_levels <- c("Pseudo_R_square", "Brier Score", 
                   "R_E","Harrell’s C", "Time Dependent Auc") #"R_sh", 
model_levels  <- c("Cox", "Cox.reduce", "Ln", "Ln.fix")

# pretty labels for display (use expressions where you want math)
metric_labels <- list(
  "Pseudo R²",
  "Brier score",
  #expression(R[sh]),
  expression(R[E]),
  "C-index",
  "AUC"
)

# reshape & summarize (keep Metric as factor with ORIGINAL levels)
df_long_mean <- combine_output %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(model_levels),
    names_to = "Model",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  filter(Metric %in% metric_levels) %>%
  group_by(Metric, Model) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = metric_levels),
    Model  = factor(Model, levels = model_levels,
                    labels = c("Cox", "Cox (Reduced)", "Ln", "Ln (Scale = 2)"))
  )

# one plot per metric; use expression titles from metric_labels
plot_list <- lapply(seq_along(metric_levels), function(i) {
  m   <- metric_levels[i]
  lab <- metric_labels[[i]]
  df_m <- df_long_mean %>% filter(Metric == m)

  ggplot(df_m, aes(x = Model, y = mean_value)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7) +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_value, 3)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +  # add headroom for labels
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Value") +
    ggtitle(lab) +  # expression-friendly title
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 9),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 9)
    )
})

# arrange (e.g., 2 rows x 3 cols)
final_plot <- wrap_plots(plotlist = plot_list, nrow = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  )

final_plot
# Save figure
ggsave("paper.fig5.pdf", final_plot, width = 10, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
combine_output <- read_csv("paper.sim2_extra_table.csv")

# originals to filter/loop with
metric_levels <- c("Pseudo_R_square", "Brier Score", 
                   "Harrell’s C", "Time Dependent Auc") #"R_sh", 
model_levels  <- c("RF", "Cox", "Ln")

# pretty labels for display (use expressions where you want math)
metric_labels <- list(
  "Pseudo R²", 
  "Brier score",
  #expression(R[sh]),
  #expression(R[E]),
  "C-index",
  "AUC"
)

# reshape & summarize (keep Metric as factor with ORIGINAL levels)
df_long_mean <- combine_output %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(model_levels),
    names_to = "Model",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  filter(Metric %in% metric_levels) %>%
  group_by(Metric, Model) %>%
  summarise(mean_value = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = metric_levels),
    Model  = factor(Model, levels = model_levels,
                    labels = c("RF", "Cox", "Ln"))
  )

# one plot per metric; use expression titles from metric_labels
plot_list <- lapply(seq_along(metric_levels), function(i) {
  m   <- metric_levels[i]
  lab <- metric_labels[[i]]
  df_m <- df_long_mean %>% filter(Metric == m)

  ggplot(df_m, aes(x = Model, y = mean_value)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.5) +
    geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_value, 3)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +  # add headroom for labels
    labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean Value") +
    ggtitle(lab) +  # expression-friendly title
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
      plot.title  = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 9),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 9)
    )
})

# arrange (e.g., 2 rows x 3 cols)
final_plot <- wrap_plots(plotlist = plot_list, nrow = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  )

final_plot
# Save figure
ggsave("paper.fig5.2.pdf", final_plot, width = 5.5, height = 3.5, dpi = 300)
```

## sample simulation

```{r, message=FALSE}
### test data
sim_table_sample <- expand.grid(n = c(300, 3000),
                                censore = c(0.0, 0.3, 0.7),
                                v = 1,
                                beta = 1,
                                index = c(1:100))
sim_table_sample$index <- 1:dim(sim_table_sample)[1]
sim_table_sample <- left_join(sim_table_sample, sim_model, by = c("v", "beta"))

sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = 10000, pi_c = 0.00,
                         v = 1, beta = c(1, -1), seed = 1234) -> df_ref
t_50 <- median(df_ref$time)
t_90 <- quantile(df_ref$time, 0.9) %>% as.numeric()

output_file <-  "paper.sim3_table.csv"
apply(sim_table_sample, 1, function(x){
  sim_cox_weibull_censored(n = x[["n"]], pi_c = x[["censore"]],
                           v = x[["v"]], beta = x[["beta"]]*c(1, -1), 
                           seed = x[["index"]]) -> df
  m.cox.index <- x[["model"]]
  m.cox <- model_list[[m.cox.index]][["m.cox"]]
  covs <- names(m.cox$coefficients)
  pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox, covs = covs, 
                                new_data = df, tau = t_50)
  pam.summary(list(value=pred1), tau = t_50, t_star = t_50) -> table_out1
  table_out1$index <- x[["index"]]
  table_out1$n <- x[["n"]]
  table_out1$censore <- x[["censore"]]
  table_out1$model <- x[["model"]]
  table_out1$quantile <- 50
  
  pred2 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox, covs = covs, 
                                new_data = df, tau = t_90)
  pam.summary(list(value=pred2), tau = t_90, t_star = t_90) -> table_out2
  table_out2$index <- x[["index"]]
  table_out2$n <- x[["n"]]
  table_out2$censore <- x[["censore"]]
  table_out2$model <- x[["model"]]
  table_out2$quantile <- 90
  
  table_out <- rbind(table_out1, table_out2)
  
  if (!file.exists(output_file)) {
    # if file doesn't exist → write header
    write.csv(table_out, file = output_file, row.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    # if file exists → append without header
    write.table(
      table_out,
      file = output_file,
      sep = ",",
      row.names = FALSE,
      col.names = FALSE,
      append = TRUE
    )
  }
  
  return(table_out)
}) -> data_list
```


```{r, message=FALSE}
ref_output <- read_csv("paper.sim3_table.csv") %>%
  group_by(Metric, n, censore, quantile) %>% 
  summarise(ref = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3), .groups = "drop") %>% 
  filter(n == 3000, censore == 0) %>% 
  select(-c(n, censore))
sample_output <- read_csv("paper.sim3_table.csv") %>%
  left_join(ref_output, by = c("Metric", "quantile")) %>% 
  mutate(bias = value - ref, quantile = factor(quantile, levels = c(90, 50)))

# --- Data prep ---

# 1) Choose the metrics to include (and their order)
metrics_to_plot <- c("Pseudo_R_square", "Brier Score", "R_E",  #"R_sh",
                     "Harrell’s C", "Uno’s C", "Time Dependent Auc")

# 2) Pretty labels (mix of strings + plotmath expressions is fine)
metric_pretty <- list(
  "Pseudo_R_square"   = expression("Pseudo R"^2),
  "Brier Score"       = "Brier score",
  #"R_sh"              = expression(R[sh]),
  "R_E"               = expression(R[E]),
  "Harrell’s C"       = "Harrell’s C",
  "Uno’s C"           = "Uno’s C",
  "Time Dependent Auc"= "AUC"
)


df <- sample_output %>%
  filter(Metric %in% metrics_to_plot) %>%
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = metrics_to_plot),
    cens_pct = paste0(round(censore * 100), "%"),
    n = as.numeric(n)
  )

y_limits <- df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Metric) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    ymin = min(bias, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax = max(bias, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Metric = as.character(Metric))

metrics <- levels(df$Metric)
cens_levels <- sort(unique(df$censore))
row_tags <- letters[seq_along(cens_levels)]
row_labels <- paste0("(", row_tags, ") censoring ", 
                     paste0(round(cens_levels * 100), "%"))

# ---- Update the small-plot maker to use pretty labels ----
make_metric_boxplot <- function(dat, metric_name, show_xlab = FALSE, show_ylab = TRUE) {
  m_chr <- as.character(metric_name)
  lim_row <- y_limits[y_limits$Metric == m_chr, ]
  ylims   <- c(lim_row$ymin, lim_row$ymax)

  # Pick pretty label (expression or string)
  y_lab <- if (show_ylab) metric_pretty[[m_chr]] else NULL

  ggplot(dat %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == metric_name),
         aes(x = factor(n), y = bias, fill = quantile)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_boxplot(
      outlier.shape = NA, 
      width = 0.6, 
      color = "black",        # consistent outline
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)
    ) +
    # --- Fill colors: one gray, one white (hollow-like) ---
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c("90" = "gray70", "50" = "white"),  # color-blind friendly
      name = "Quantile",
      labels = c("90%", "50%")
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = ylims, 
                       expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.05))) +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = y_lab
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(6, 6, 6, 6),
      axis.title.y = if (show_ylab) element_text() else element_blank()
    )
}

# --- Assemble all rows ---
row_grobs <- lapply(seq_along(cens_levels), function(i) {
  # For the last row, show x-axis labels; otherwise, hide them
  show_xlab <- (i == length(cens_levels))
  
  # Modify make_metric_boxplot call to accept show_xlab info
  build_row_mod <- function(cens_val, row_lab, show_xlab_row) {
    row_data <- df %>% dplyr::filter(censore == cens_val)
    lab_plot <- ggplot() +
      annotate("text",
               x = 0, y = 1, label = row_lab,
               hjust = -0.2, vjust = -1.5,
               fontface = "bold", size = 4.8) +
      xlim(0, 1) + ylim(0, 1) +
      coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
      theme_void(base_size = 12) +
      theme(plot.margin = margin(t = 22, r = 16, b = 4, l = 14))
    
    mid_id <- ceiling(length(metrics) / 2)
    metric_plots <- lapply(seq_along(metrics), function(i) {
      make_metric_boxplot(
        dat = row_data,
        metric_name = metrics[i],
        show_xlab = show_xlab_row && (i == mid_id)
      )
    })
    
    patchwork::wrap_plots(
      c(list(lab_plot), metric_plots),
      ncol = length(metrics) + 1,
      widths = c(0.1, rep(1, length(metrics)))
    )
  }
  
  build_row_mod(cens_levels[i], row_labels[i], show_xlab)
})

# --- Combine ---
big_figure <- Reduce(`/`, row_grobs) +
  plot_layout(heights = rep(1, length(row_grobs))) &
  theme(legend.position = "none")


x_label <- ggplot() +
  annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.5,
           label = "Sample size", size = 5) +
  theme_void()

final_plot <- wrap_plots(
  big_figure,
  x_label,
  ncol = 1,
  heights = c(1, 0.05)  # adjust spacing
)

final_plot

# Save figure
ggsave("paper.fig6.pdf", final_plot, width = 13, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```



# Real data
## Right-censored

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
set.seed(1234567)
data(pbc, package = "survival")
df <- pbc %>% 
  filter(is.na(trt)==F) %>% 
  mutate(log_albumin = log(albumin),
         log_bili = log(bili),
         log_protime = log(protime),
         status = ifelse(status==2, 1, 0)) %>% 
  select(time, status, age, log_albumin, log_bili, log_protime, edema)
covs <- names(df)[-c(1:2)]
covs2 <- covs[c(1,5)]
train_df_idx <- sample(1:dim(df)[1], round(2/3*dim(df)[1]))
train_df <- df[train_df_idx, ]
train_df2 <- df[train_df_idx, c(names(df)[c(1:2)], covs)]
test_df <- df[-train_df_idx, ]
test_df2 <- df[-train_df_idx, c(names(df)[c(1:2)], covs)]

## model 
m.cox1 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = train_df, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)
m.cox2 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = train_df[,c(1:2,3,7)], x = TRUE, y = TRUE)
m.wei1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ ., 
                   data = train_df, dist="weibull", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
m.wei2 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ .,  scale = 5,
                   data = train_df, dist="weibull", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
m.ln1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ ., 
                   data = train_df, dist="lognormal", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
## predict prob

pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df) 
pred2 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox2, covs = covs2, new_data = test_df2)
pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df) 
pred4 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei2, covs = covs, new_data = test_df) 
pred5 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df) 
```


```{r}
mods <- list(
  Cox = pred1,
  Cox.Reduce = pred2,
  Weibull.AFT = pred3 #, Weibull.AFT.fix = pred4
)
pam.summary(mods, tau = 10e10)
```


```{r}
summary_pred_plot(
  data_list = mods, ncol = 3,
  titles = c("Cox PH", "Cox PH (Reduced)", 
             "Weibull AFT"), 
  invert_linear = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE) 
)
ggsave("paper.fig1.pdf", width = 9, height = 3.5)
```


### for talk slides

```{r}
set.seed(1234567)
data(pbc, package = "survival")
df <- pbc %>% 
  filter(is.na(trt)==F) %>% 
  mutate(log_albumin = log(albumin),
         log_bili = log(bili),
         log_protime = log(protime),
         status = ifelse(status==2, 1, 0)) %>% 
  select(time, status, age, log_albumin, log_bili, log_protime, edema)
covs <- names(df)[-c(1:2)]
covs2 <- covs[c(1,5)]
train_df_idx <- sample(1:dim(df)[1], round(2/3*dim(df)[1]))
train_df <- df[train_df_idx, ]
train_df2 <- df[train_df_idx, c(names(df)[c(1:2)], covs)]
test_df <- df[-train_df_idx, ]
test_df2 <- df[-train_df_idx, c(names(df)[c(1:2)], covs)]

## model 
m.cox1 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = train_df, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)
m.cox2 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = train_df[,c(1:2,3,7)], x = TRUE, y = TRUE)
m.wei1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ ., 
                   data = train_df, dist="weibull", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
m.wei2 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ .,  scale = 5,
                   data = train_df, dist="weibull", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)
m.ln1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status) ~ ., 
                   data = train_df, dist="lognormal", x=TRUE, y=TRUE)

tau <- 10e10
pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau)  
pred5 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
mods <- list(
  Cox = pred1,
  Weibull.AFT = pred3,
  Lognomal.AFT = pred5
)
pam.summary(mods, tau = tau)

tau <- 4459
pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau)  
pred5 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
mods <- list(
  Cox = pred1,
  Weibull.AFT = pred3,
  Lognomal.AFT = pred5
)
pam.summary(mods, tau = tau)
summary_pred_plot(
  data_list = mods, ncol = 3,
  titles = c("Cox PH", "wb", 
             "ln"), 
  invert_linear = c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE) 
)
tau <- 2000
pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau)  
pred5 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
mods <- list(
  Cox = pred1,
  Weibull.AFT = pred3,
  Lognomal.AFT = pred5
)
pam.summary(mods, tau = tau)
summary_pred_plot(
  data_list = mods, ncol = 3,
  titles = c("Cox PH", "wb", 
             "ln"), 
  invert_linear = c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE) 
)
tau <- 1000
pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau)  
pred5 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
mods <- list(
  Cox = pred1,
  Weibull.AFT = pred3,
  Lognomal.AFT = pred5
)
pam.summary(mods, tau = tau)

tau <- 10e10
pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = m.cox1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
pred3 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.wei1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau)  
pred5 <- pam.surverg_restricted(model = m.ln1, covs = covs, new_data = test_df, tau = tau) 
mods <- list(
  Cox = pred1,
  Weibull.AFT = pred3,
  Lognomal.AFT = pred5
)
results <- NULL
metrics_list <- c("Pseudo_R2_point", "Harrell’s C", "Uno’s C", 
                  "Brier Score", "Time Dependent Auc")
for(i in 1:5){
  for(t in seq(1000, 5000, length.out=11)){
    pam.summary(mods, tau = t, t_star = t, 
                metrics = metrics_list[i])->l
    temp_out <- data.frame(l, t=t)
    results <- rbind(results, temp_out)
  }
}

```


## Competing-risk

```{r}
library(cmprsk)
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(1234567)
data(framingham, package = "riskCommunicator")
df_ref <- framingham %>% filter(PERIOD==1) %>% 
  select(DEATH, TIMEDTH, HYPERTEN, TIMEHYP, 
         RANDID, SEX:educ, RANDID, TIME) %>% 
  group_by(RANDID) %>% 
  mutate(time = min(TIMEDTH, TIMEHYP),
         status = ifelse(time==TIMEDTH, DEATH*2, HYPERTEN)) %>% 
  select(time, status, everything()) %>% 
  filter(time!=0) %>% ungroup() %>% 
  select(time, status, RANDID) %>% na.omit() #SEX:educ, -BPMEDS

df <- df_ref %>% 
  left_join(framingham %>% 
              select(RANDID, TIME, PERIOD, SEX:educ, -BPMEDS), 
            by = "RANDID") %>%
  na.omit() %>% 
  filter(time > TIME) %>% 
  group_by(RANDID) %>% 
  slice(n()) %>% 
  ungroup() %>% 
  mutate(time = time - TIME) %>% 
  select(-c(RANDID, TIME, PERIOD))

covs <- names(df)[-c(1:2)]
covs2 <- covs[c(1:3, 5)]
train_df_idx <- sample(1:dim(df)[1], round(2/3*dim(df)[1]))
train_df <- df[train_df_idx, ]
train_df2 <- df[train_df_idx, c(names(df)[c(1:2)], covs2)]
test_df <- df[-train_df_idx, ]
test_df2 <- df[-train_df_idx, c(names(df)[c(1:2)], covs2)]

m.fg1 <- crr(train_df$time, train_df$status, train_df[, covs, drop = FALSE])
m.fg2 <- crr(train_df$time, train_df$status, train_df[, covs2, drop = FALSE])
m.aft1 <- survreg(Surv(time, status==1) ~.,
                      data = train_df, x = T, dist="weibull")
m.aft2 <- survreg(Surv(time, status==2) ~., 
                      data = train_df, x = T, dist="weibull")
m.aft3 <- survreg(Surv(time, status==1) ~., scale = 5,
                      data = train_df, x = T, dist="weibull")
m.aft4 <- survreg(Surv(time, status==2) ~., scale = 5,
                      data = train_df, x = T, dist="weibull")

pred1 <- pam.predict_cr(fg_model = m.fg1, newdata = test_df, covs = covs, tau = 5475)
pred2 <- pam.predict_cr(fg_model = m.fg2, newdata = test_df2, covs = covs2, tau = 5475)
pred3 <- pam.predict_cr(model1 = m.aft1, model2 = m.aft2, newdata = test_df, covs = covs, tau = 5475)
pred4 <- pam.predict_cr(model1 = m.aft3, model2 = m.aft4, newdata = test_df, covs = covs, tau = 5475)

mods_cr <- list(
  FG = pred1,
  FG.Reduced = pred2,
  CSH.AFT = pred3,
  CSH.AFT.FIX = pred4
)

pam.summary_cr(mods_cr, event_type = 1, tau = 5475, t_star = 5475)
```


```{r}
summary_pred_plot(
  mods_cr,
  titles = c("Fine–Gray", "Fine–Gray (Reduced)", 
             "CSH Weibull AFT", "CSH Weibull AFT (Scale = 5)"),
  invert_linear = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE),
  label_name = c("Censored", "Event of interest", "Competing event"),
  ncol = 2,
  sample_size = 200,
  restrict_time = 5475
)
ggsave("paper.fig2.pdf", width = 9, height = 7)
```


## Sample design

```{r}
library(survival)
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(2025)
data(GBSG2, package="pec")
df <- GBSG2 %>% 
    mutate(status = cens,
           id = row_number()) %>% 
    filter(time!=0) %>% 
    select(time, status, id, horTh:estrec) %>% na.omit()

covs <- names(df)[-c(1:3)]
train_df_idx <- sample(1:dim(df)[1], round(2/3*dim(df)[1]))
train_df <- df[train_df_idx, ]
test_df <- df[-train_df_idx, ]

### Unstratified case-cohort 
subco_size <- 0.10                     
subcohort  <- sample(train_df$id, round(nrow(train_df) * subco_size))
sub.test  <- sample(test_df$id, round(nrow(test_df) * subco_size))
m.km <- survfit(Surv(time, 1 - status) ~ 1, data = train_df)

cc_train <- train_df %>% mutate(subco = id %in% subcohort, case = status == 1)
cc_train$weights <- cc_weights(time = cc_train$time, 
                               status = cc_train$status,
                               subcohort = cc_train$subco)
cc_train <- cc_train %>% filter(subco | status == 1)
train_weights <- cc_train$weights
cc_train <- cc_train %>% select(time, status, all_of(covs))
cc_train2 <- train_df %>% 
  filter(id %in% sample(train_df$id, dim(cc_train)[1])) %>% 
  select(time, status, all_of(covs))

cc_test <- test_df %>% mutate(subco = id %in% sub.test, case = status == 1)
cc_test$weights <- cc_weights(time = cc_test$time,
                              status = cc_test$status,
                              subcohort = cc_test$subco)
cc_test <- cc_test %>% filter(subco | status == 1)
test_weights <- cc_test$weights
cc_test <- cc_test %>% select(time, status, all_of(covs))


fit_cox1 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~., data = cc_train,
                  weights = train_weights, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)
fit_cox2 <- coxph(Surv(time, status) ~ ., data = cc_train2, x = TRUE, y = TRUE)
pred1 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = fit_cox1, covs = covs, new_data = cc_test)
pred2 <- pam.coxph_restricted(model = fit_cox2, covs = covs, new_data = cc_test)

mods <- list(
  Cox.cc = pred1,
  Cox.random = pred2
)
pam.sample_design(models = mods, km_cens = m.km, case_weights = test_weights)
```


```{r}
summary_pred_plot(
  data_list = mods,
  titles    = c("Cox (Case-cohort)", "Cox (Random sample)"),
  ncol      = 2,
  tag_levels = "a",          
  invert_linear = c(FALSE, FALSE) 
)
ggsave("paper.fig3.pdf", width = 9, height = 4)
```
